sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.81 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: A topological group is said to be$F$-balanced\/ if for any subset$X$and any neighborhood of the identity$U$there is a neighborhood of the identity$V$such that$VX XU$. Is every$F$-balanced group balanced, that is, does it have a basis of neighborhoods of the identity consisting of invariant sets?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
